issues: Claringbull is described as being from 'St. Lawrence, Newfoundland' which the OCR confirms, but the draft fails to note he moved to Pincher Creek, Alta. at age 10 — this is consistent with the known roster fact of Pincher Creek, AB.
The draft states Claringbull is 'completing commercial pilot training' but the OCR says he has 'two weeks remaining in his commercial pilot training' — a minor but more precise detail.

The draft mentions he 'trained with the Montreal Canadiens organization' which is correct, but omits that he was drafted by Montreal and attended two training camps before being offered a contract he turned down — key narrative details.

The long description says the article discusses his passion for flying 'At the 1987 Centennial Cup' which is correct context, but the draft does not mention the adjacent-column noise: there is a separate short news item at the top of the OCR about Pembroke Lumber Kings beating Dartmouth 8-4, which is unrelated to the Claringbull profile and should not be described.

The draft description is otherwise accurate in its core facts but could be more precise about the summer job at Vancouver International Airport and his ultimate airline goal vs. immediate bush pilot goal.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Profile of Richmond Sockeyes defenceman Mike Claringbull, who moved from Pincher Creek to pursue aviation over pro hockey after two Montreal Canadiens training camps.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: At the 1987 Centennial Cup in Humboldt, Saskatchewan, Richmond Sockeyes defenceman Mike Claringbull explains why he chose a flying career over professional hockey. A native of St. Lawrence, Newfoundland who moved to Pincher Creek, Alberta at age 10, Claringbull played for the Medicine Hat Tigers and attended two Montreal Canadiens training camps before declining a contract offer. With two weeks remaining in his commercial pilot training and a summer job lined up at Vancouver International Airport, Claringbull says his immediate goal is to become a bush pilot, with a longer-term aim of flying for a major airline.
review.correctedHeadline: Sockeye vet has sights set on career in air
review.notes: The OCR contains adjacent-column noise at the top: a separate game recap about Pembroke Lumber Kings defeating Dartmouth Fuel Kids 8-4 to advance in the Centennial Cup round-robin. This is tangentially Sockeyes-relevant (it mentions the Richmond–Humboldt winner gets a bye) but the main Sockeyes article is clearly the Claringbull profile. The draft headline and byline are correct. The round in which Claringbull was drafted by Montreal is OCR-garbled (reads "I was drafted in the round") — draft wisely omits the specific round number.
